channel.title: dreamclerk blog
channel.description: field notes from building dreamclerk. the data, the rejects, the rubric changes. nothing polished after the fact.

item.title: the 90-second internship interview that changed 14% of outcomes
item.dc:creator: dreamclerk team
item.description: we ran a 90-second phone screen on 1,200 internship applicants. 14% got a callback. here is what we changed, and what we got wrong.
item.content:encoded: we ran a 90-second phone screen on 1,200 internship applicants. 14% got a callback. here is what we changed, and what we got wrong.

item.title: inside our bias audit — the rubric, the data, the changes
item.dc:creator: dreamclerk team
item.description: every quarter we run a bias audit on the dreamclerk interview. here is the rubric, the per-group pass-rates, and the three rubric changes we made after cohort 1.
item.content:encoded: every quarter we run a bias audit on the dreamclerk interview. here is the rubric, the per-group pass-rates, and the three rubric changes we made after cohort 1.

item.title: how to pass a coding interview with no experience
item.dc:creator: dreamclerk team
item.description: no internships. no github. no leetcode streak. here is the 4-step protocol we built to prep dreamclerk applicants in 6 weeks — and what the data says about it.
item.content:encoded: no internships. no github. no leetcode streak. here is the 4-step protocol we built to prep dreamclerk applicants in 6 weeks — and what the data says about it.

item.title: the in-browser ide — what it actually runs, what it can't
item.dc:creator: dreamclerk team
item.description: monaco on top of a webcontainer. 200ms cold start. 12mb of wasm. here is the architecture, the security model, and the 3 things it cannot do.
item.content:encoded: monaco on top of a webcontainer. 200ms cold start. 12mb of wasm. here is the architecture, the security model, and the 3 things it cannot do.

item.title: shipping code vs knowing code — a 5-minute glossary
item.dc:creator: dreamclerk team
item.description: the 14 terms every dreamclerk applicant should be able to use in a sentence, with a worked example for each. bookmark this. re-read it before the interview.
item.content:encoded: the 14 terms every dreamclerk applicant should be able to use in a sentence, with a worked example for each. bookmark this. re-read it before the interview.

item.title: fresher unemployment in india 2026 — the numbers, the cause, and the one fix that works
item.dc:creator: dreamclerk team
item.description: 73% of indian engineering graduates are unemployed a year after college. we break down the 2026 numbers, the four reasons companies reject freshers, and the one fix that is actually moving the needle.
item.content:encoded: 73% of indian engineering graduates are unemployed a year after college. we break down the 2026 numbers, the four reasons companies reject freshers, and the one fix that is actually moving the needle.

item.title: the 2-year experience trap — why the requirement exists, and what it actually buys
item.dc:creator: dreamclerk team
item.description: 85% of indian tech JDs ask for 2+ years experience. only 12% of applicants have it. the gap is structural, not preference. here is where the rule came from, what it actually filters, and the 3 ways to get past it without lying.
item.content:encoded: 85% of indian tech JDs ask for 2+ years experience. only 12% of applicants have it. the gap is structural, not preference. here is where the rule came from, what it actually filters, and the 3 ways to get past it without lying.

item.title: how to get hired as a fresher with no internship and no network — a 6-week playbook
item.dc:creator: dreamclerk team
item.description: no internship. no github. no alumni network. no tier-1 college. here is the exact 6-week protocol that 187 dreamclerk applicants used to go from 14% interview rate to 31%, with the calendar, the artifact list, and the rejection log included.
item.content:encoded: no internship. no github. no alumni network. no tier-1 college. here is the exact 6-week protocol that 187 dreamclerk applicants used to go from 14% interview rate to 31%, with the calendar, the artifact list, and the rejection log included.

item.title: why "2 years experience required" is a tax on your future engineering team
item.dc:creator: dreamclerk team
item.description: a hiring manager writes. the rule is a fossil. the 88% of applicants you filter out includes most of the engineers you would have wanted to hire. here is the data, the math, and the 4-step replacement.
item.content:encoded: a hiring manager writes. the rule is a fossil. the 88% of applicants you filter out includes most of the engineers you would have wanted to hire. here is the data, the math, and the 4-step replacement.

item.title: the resume is dead — 3 signals that actually predict a good hire in 2026
item.dc:creator: dreamclerk team
item.description: the resume predicts 6-month retention at r=0.12. three other signals predict it at r=0.40, r=0.38, and r=0.34. here is what they are, why they work, and how to build all three in 90 days without a tier-1 college or a brand-name internship.
item.content:encoded: the resume predicts 6-month retention at r=0.12. three other signals predict it at r=0.40, r=0.38, and r=0.34. here is what they are, why they work, and how to build all three in 90 days without a tier-1 college or a brand-name internship.
